rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior product-recall research that focused on business/marketing outcomes, and the cited paper is a core product-harm crisis study on brand-equity effects and moderating mechanisms (consumer expectations). Given this strong topical fit and plausible foundational role in the recall/crisis literature, this is not clearly an incidental mention. The pair is directly DOI-matched in the reference list (10.1509/jmkr.37.2.215.18729).
